content: agent1: implementing 'Add Token Position Tracking' feature - adding return_positions param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will touch encode() and possibly _encode_ordinary_native/_encode_native helper logic. Let me know if you're touching core.py too.

agent1: my change is scoped to encode() only in tiktoken/core.py - adding 'return_positions: bool = False' kwarg at the end of the signature, plus a new private helper method _encode_with_positions near the other _encode_* helpers at the bottom of the class. I won't touch encode_ordinary or other methods. If you're adding 'transformers' to encode() too, let's add params in the same edit to avoid clobbering - can you tell me the exact param name/position you're adding so I place mine after it, or shall I go first and you rebase on top?

agent1: done. Wrote patch.txt for return_positions feature in encode() (tiktoken/core.py) - added return_positions kwarg + new _encode_with_positions private helper. Verified against plain encode() output, special tokens, UTF-8 multi-byte chars, and full pytest suite (32 passed). Working tree diff is scoped to just those additions, no conflicts observed with your transformers change (git status shows no transformers edits present in my copy of the file — let me know if that's unexpected).
